query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Conspirator', 'A person who plans to do something unlawful or harmful', 'Over time, the community learned to conspirator its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Conspire', 'To plot something unlawful or harmful', 'The author hoped the new policy would conspire tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Constable', 'A police officer ranking below sergeant (british)', 'The essay highlights the constable of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Constellation', 'An arbitrary formation of stars perceived as a figure', 'The essay highlights the constellation of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Consternation', 'Feelings of anxiety or dismay', 'Her consternation became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Constituency', 'A district represented by one or more elected officials', 'The essay highlights the constituency of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Constituent', 'Part of a whole', 'The reviewer described the tone as constituent, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Constrain', 'To limit or restrict', '“Time constraints forced them to work faster.”', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Constrict', 'To make narrower, especially by encircling pressure', 'Over time, the community learned to constrict its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Construe', 'To interpret in a particular way', 'The author hoped the new policy would construe tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Consul', 'Official in a country who protects their nation''s interests', 'Her consul became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Consulate', 'The residency of a consul', 'Scholars noted a striking consulate between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Consummate', 'To bring to a state of perfectio, fulfill', 'The author hoped the new policy would consummate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Consumption', 'The act of consuming something', 'The essay highlights the consumption of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Consumptive', 'Tending to consume, destructive, wasteful', 'What seemed consumptive at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Contagion', 'A disease spread with or without contact', 'Scholars noted a striking contagion between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Contagious', 'Of a disease, easily transmitted to others', 'The author hoped the new policy would contagious tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Contaminate', 'To introduce impurities or foreign matter', 'Over time, the community learned to contaminate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Contemplate', 'To ponder or consider', 'Over time, the community learned to contemplate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Contemporaneous', 'Existing or occurring in the same period of time', 'The reviewer described the tone as contemporaneous, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Contemporary', 'From the same time period, coexistent in time', 'The essay highlights the contemporary of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Contempt', 'Feeling that a person or a thing is beneath consideration', 'The essay highlights the contempt of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Contemptible', 'Deserving of or held in contempt,  despicable', 'Her contemptible became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Contemptuous', 'Scornful, disdainful', 'The passage presents a contemptuous portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Contend', 'To argue or compete', '“She contended that the new policy was unfair.”', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Contender', 'Someone who competes with one or more other people', 'Her contender became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Contention', 'A disagreement or argument', '“The main point of contention was the budget proposal.”', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Contentious', 'Causing or likely to cause an argument; controversial', 'Over time, the community learned to contentious its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Contentment', 'A state of happiness and satisfaction', 'Scholars noted a striking contentment between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Context', 'The circumstances that form the setting of an idea', 'Her context became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Contextualize', 'To provide background or context', '“The professor contextualized the historical event for the students.”', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Contiguity', 'Contact or proximity', 'The essay highlights the contiguity of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Contiguous', 'Connected, touching, abutting', 'The passage presents a contiguous portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Continence', 'The exercise of self constraint in sexual matters', 'Scholars noted a striking continence between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Contingency', 'Dependence on chance or the fulfillment of a condition', 'Her contingency became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Contingent', 'Dependent on certain conditions', '“Her promotion was contingent on her performance.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Continuance', 'The state of remaining in existence or operation', 'The essay highlights the continuance of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Continuator', 'A person or thing that continues', 'Scholars noted a striking continuator between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Continuity', 'The consistency of parts of a story or elements in a series across different episodes', 'The essay highlights the continuity of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Contort', 'To twist in a violent manner', 'Critics argued that the plan might contort the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Contraband', 'Prohibited from being traded', 'Scholars noted a striking contraband between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Contradict', 'To go against or oppose', '“His statement contradicted the facts.”', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Contradiction', 'A person, thing, or idea in which has inconsistent elements', 'The essay highlights the contradiction of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Contradictory', 'Mutually opposed or inconsistent', 'The essay highlights the contradictory of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Contraposition', 'Placement opposite or against', 'The essay highlights the contraposition of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Contravene', 'To act contrary to an order, or fail to conform', 'The author hoped the new policy would contravene tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Contretemp', 'An unforeseen, inopportune, or embarrassing event, a hitch', 'The essay highlights the contretemp of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Contribution', 'Something given or offered that adds to a larger whole', 'Critics argued that the plan might contribution the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Contributor', 'A benefactor, someone who donates to charity or some cause', 'The author hoped the new policy would contributor tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Contrite', 'Feeling remorse or guilt', 'The passage presents a contrite portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
